The '''''Suprosa''''' was a heavily modified [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[MB-C1 medium transport]].

==History==
Allegedly hauling fertilizer, the ''Suprosa'' ran under [[Xizor Transport Systems/Legends|Xizor Transport Systems]] contract.<ref name="secrets">''[[The Secrets of Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]''</ref> The intelligence indicating that ''Suprosa'' carried valuable information was leaked deliberately by [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]], as part of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] ruse to lure the Alliance to [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] in order to obliterate them.<ref name="SEnovel">[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' novel]]</ref>

It was [[Raid on the Suprosa|raided]] by members of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]],<ref name="SE4">[[Shadows of the Empire 4|''Shadows of the Empire'' 4]]</ref> including [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Dash Rendar/Legends|Dash Rendar]], [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace Azzameen]] and a group of Bothans piloting [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wings]]. They found it to be transporting [[Death Star II plans|data]] on the construction of the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]].<ref name="XWA" /><ref name="secrets" /><ref name="SEnovel" /><ref name="Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire Ertl minicomic">[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire Ertl minicomic|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' Ertl minicomic]]</ref><ref name="Card-Topps-Attack on the Suprosa">{{Topps|set=1996 Topps Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|cardname=Attack on the ''Suprosa''|link=https://www.rebelscum.com/photo.asp?image=/sote/soteTC033.jpg}}</ref>

Many of the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]] died in the effort when ''Suprosa'' launched a [[diamond boron missile]] into the Y-wing formation, obliterating everything in a 500-meter radius. However, the Rebel forces were able to disable the ship. Strafing the ship with his Y-wing, Azzameen disabled the transport's weapons with his [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]] before another missile could be fired. Skywalker then disabled the ship's engines with his X-wing fighter.<ref name="XWA" /> Luke briefly felt the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] during his attack run on the freighter's engines in response to half his Bothan squadron being destroyed.

With the vessel disabled, Dash and several Bothans boarded the ship to secure the information.<ref name="XWA" /> After fighting [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] and a [[Loader droid/Legends|loader droid]] he downloaded the plans.<ref name="Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire video game">[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (video game)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' video game]]</ref><ref name="Card-Topps-Dash Aboard the Suprosa">{{Topps|set=1996 Topps Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|cardname=Dash Aboard the ''Suprosa''|link=https://www.rebelscum.com/photo.asp?image=/sote/soteTC097.jpg}}</ref>

The information was taken to [[Kothlis/Legends|Kothlis]] to be decoded.<ref name="SE4" /><ref name="SEnovel" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Suprosasketch.jpg|thumb|250px|Early concept sketch of the freighter by [[Jon Knoles]], along with a human figure and the ''Outrider''.]]
The [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Shadows of the Empire'' novel]] identifies ''Suprosa'' as a [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] freighter.

In the ''Shadows of the Empire'' novel, there was not enough time to notify Rebel command of the "Endor Project" plans being transferred on the ''Suprosa''; the novel implies that other than Luke Skywalker and Dash Rendar, all of the pilots present were Bothans and Luke is credited with disabling the ship by himself. In the video game ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'', the Alliance comes to support, with Ace incapacitating the freighter. For this article, both are assumed to be correct, with Ace keeping the ship from firing its weapons again, and Luke disabling the ship's engines.

The ''Shadows of the Empire'' level taking place in the ''Suprosa'' makes the ship look substantially larger than it seems from the exterior. It also has a [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]], and Dash has to open it so that the [[Outrider/Legends|''Outrider'']] enters.
